subject: Grade 8 Cross-Curricular
grade_subject_fit: Reusable Grade 8 planning toolkit for Math 8, Science 8, ELA 8, Career Education 8, and ADST 8.
unit_context: Teacher-facing toolkit for building future packages from curriculum target, context family, student product, support version, and assessment evidence.
assignment_purpose: Teachers use compact curriculum target cards to keep future packages aligned before artifact production begins.
final_evidence_target: A future package passoff that names the target, context, product, support version, evidence, and separated teacher/student artifacts.
student_task_flow: Choose or receive a matching context.
Complete the done-when checklist and completion_check.
success_criteria: Teacher and student materials are separated.
supports_scaffolds: Use target cards to reduce planning drift.
Use K-support moves that preserve the learning goal.
Use the student support frame for reduced-load versions.

differentiation_model.support_pathway: Use a preselected target, one context family, and the student support frame.
differentiation_model.core_pathway: Choose target, context, product, support version, and assessment evidence.
differentiation_model.extension_pathway: Compare two context families and justify which better protects the target.

teacher_implementation_notes: This toolkit is for planning future packages.
Garden / planter / food systems is one option, not the default.
For First Peoples content, use teacher-selected/local-approved sources only.
likely_misconceptions: Context can become decoration if the target is not named first.
Student choice can hide the learning goal.
Support can accidentally lower the target instead of reducing barriers.

teacher_guide.title: Teacher Guide - Grade 8 Curriculum Target Card Toolkit
teacher_guide.overview: Use this toolkit to build Grade 8 packages from curriculum target + context + student product + support version + assessment evidence.
teacher_guide.essential_question: How do we keep future packages aligned to the target instead of being driven by a favourite context?
teacher_guide.opening_frame: Start with the curriculum target. Choose the context only after the target and evidence are clear.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Build a Grade 8 package by choosing a curriculum target first, then selecting a context family, student product, support version, and assessment evidence.
materials: quick_tools: Do not default everything to garden or planter context.
quick_tools: Keep answer keys and teacher look-fors out of student-facing materials.
quick_tools: Support versions preserve the learning goal and reduce access barriers.
quick_tools: Every student packet needs a done-when checklist or completion_check signal.

model.title: Teacher model
model.sample: Target: unit rates. Context: consumer/money. Product: best-buy recommendation. Support: partial table. Evidence: unit-rate comparison and explanation.

teacher_guide.assessment_focus: assessment_look_fors: selected context supports the target; product shows evidence; K-support preserves the same learning goal; teacher/student materials are separated; artifact roles are declared clearly.
teacher_notes: teacher_notes: Garden / planter / food systems is one option, not the default.
teacher_notes: Use local-approved sources for First Peoples content.

toolkit_overview.title: Toolkit Overview - Grade 8 Curriculum Target Cards
toolkit_overview.purpose_line: Teacher-facing overview for using target cards before fixture creation.
instructions: Use the planning chain before writing artifacts.

tasks.label: Planning chain
tasks.prompt: curriculum target + context + student product + support version + assessment evidence

tasks.label: Context guardrail
tasks.prompt: Garden / planter / food systems is one optional context family only.

tasks.label: Separation guardrail
tasks.prompt: Teacher-facing material may include notes and look-fors. Student-facing material must not include answer keys, expected answers, teacher notes, assessment look-fors, common misconceptions, or sample solutions.

embedded_supports: Use target cards to avoid package drift.
Use the artifact checklist before rendering.

context_menu.title: Context Menu - Grade 8 Package Context Families
context_menu.prompt: Use one of these context families after the curriculum target is clear: Garden / Planter / Food Systems; Design / Build / Trades; Community / Kamloops / Local Work; Outdoor Place / Land / Environment; Health / Body / Choices; Digital / Coding / Data; Consumer / Money / Market; Stories / Identity / Growth.
success_criteria: Context does not replace the target.
Garden / planter / food systems is optional, not default.

blank_curriculum_target_card_template.title: Blank Curriculum Target Card Template
instructions: Complete one target card before building artifacts.

tasks.label: Target
tasks.prompt: [Name the curriculum target]

tasks.label: Students need to
tasks.prompt: [Plain-language learning goals]

tasks.label: Best evidence
tasks.prompt: [What students can produce to show learning]

tasks.label: Common difficulties
tasks.prompt: [Predictable Grade 8 barriers]

tasks.label: K-support moves
tasks.prompt: [Reduced-load and accessibility supports]

tasks.label: Possible contexts
tasks.prompt: [Flexible context options, not just garden]

tasks.label: Likely artifacts
tasks.prompt: [Materials that could be created later]

math8_target_card_set.title: Math 8 Target Card Set - Compact Index
math8_target_card_set.purpose_line: Teacher-facing compact Math 8 target index. Full card bank is split into reference docs.
instructions: Choose one target, then select context and evidence.

tasks.label: Number and operations
tasks.prompt: Fractions; ratios, rates, percents; financial literacy and best-buy decisions.

tasks.label: Patterns and algebra
tasks.prompt: Linear relations and growth patterns; expressions, substitution, and two-step equations.

tasks.label: Geometry and measurement
tasks.prompt: Surface area, volume, 3D measurement; Pythagorean theorem; scale drawings, views, and nets.

tasks.label: Data and probability
tasks.prompt: Data, central tendency, interpretation; probability and fair games.

tasks.label: Recovery
tasks.prompt: Math skills recovery and numeracy routines.

embedded_supports: Full Math 8 cards live in docs/curriculum-target-cards/math8.md.

science8_target_card_set.title: Science 8 Target Card Set - Compact Index
instructions: Choose one target, then select context and evidence.

tasks.label: Inquiry and life science
tasks.prompt: Scientific inquiry and CER; characteristics of life; plant and animal cells; photosynthesis and cellular respiration.

tasks.label: Ecosystems and health
tasks.prompt: Microorganisms and decomposers; immune system and disease basics.

tasks.label: Physical science
tasks.prompt: Matter and particle model; atomic theory, elements, and compounds; light, waves, and electromagnetic spectrum.

tasks.label: Earth science
tasks.prompt: Plate tectonics, earth processes, and local geology.

tasks.label: Recovery
tasks.prompt: Science skills recovery and vocabulary support.

embedded_supports: Full Science 8 cards live in docs/curriculum-target-cards/science8.md.

ela8_target_card_set.title: ELA 8 Target Card Set - Compact Index
instructions: Choose the literacy target before context.

tasks.label: Reading and response
tasks.prompt: Reading strategies for short non-fiction; summary skills; literary response; protocol-aware First Peoples text response.

tasks.label: Writing
tasks.prompt: Paragraph writing foundations; opinion and argument with evidence; personal narrative and growth writing.

tasks.label: Research and media
tasks.prompt: Research basics and source use; visual and media literacy.

tasks.label: Communication
tasks.prompt: Oral communication and discussion skills.

tasks.label: Recovery
tasks.prompt: ELA skills recovery pack.

embedded_supports: Full ELA 8 cards live in docs/curriculum-target-cards/ela8.md.

careers8_target_card_set.title: Career Education 8 Target Card Set - Compact Index
careers8_target_card_set.purpose_line: Teacher-facing compact Career Education 8 target index.
instructions: Avoid forced personal disclosure. Use choice banks and private reflection options.

tasks.label: Self and goals
tasks.prompt: Values, skills, interests, strengths; goal setting and reflection; Careers skills recovery.

tasks.label: Pathways
tasks.prompt: Guest speaker prep; local career pathways; education and training pathways.

tasks.label: Workplace readiness
tasks.prompt: Workplace skills and employability; workplace safety basics; project management and collaboration.

tasks.label: Digital and community
tasks.prompt: Digital identity and first impressions; volunteering and community contribution.

embedded_supports: Full Careers 8 cards live in docs/curriculum-target-cards/careers8.md.

adst8_target_card_set.title: ADST 8 Target Card Set - Compact Index
instructions: Choose the design or technology target first.

tasks.label: Design and making
tasks.prompt: Design thinking; technical drawing; woodwork/materials/making; entrepreneurship and marketing.

tasks.label: Safety and systems
tasks.prompt: Tool, workspace, and digital safety; robotics/sensors/automated systems.

tasks.label: Digital and media
tasks.prompt: Digital literacy and source checking; computational thinking and coding logic; media arts and visual communication.

tasks.label: Food and recovery
tasks.prompt: Food studies and local food systems; ADST skills recovery pack.

embedded_supports: Full ADST 8 cards live in docs/curriculum-target-cards/adst8.md.

package_builder_template.title: Artifact Production Passoff - Package Builder Template
package_builder_template.purpose_line: Teacher-facing passoff template for turning one target card into an engine-ready package.
instructions: Copy this structure into artifact production once the target card is selected.

tasks.label: Package setup
tasks.prompt: Package title; Grade 8; subject area; context family; curriculum target.

tasks.label: Student learning
tasks.prompt: Student-friendly learning goal; student product; assessment evidence.

tasks.label: Required artifacts
tasks.prompt: student packet; teacher guide; K-support / reduced-load version; rubric or checklist; exit ticket set; organizers needed; teacher answer key or exemplar if needed, teacher-facing only.

tasks.label: Separation
tasks.prompt: Student-facing pages listed separately from teacher guide notes, answer key, look-fors, misconceptions, adaptations, and extensions.

embedded_supports: No answer leakage into student materials.
success_criteria: Student and teacher outputs are separated.

artifact_checklist.title: Artifact Checklist - Grade 8 Package Readiness
instructions: Use before generating fixtures and after rendering.

tasks.label: Curriculum target card
tasks.prompt: target is clear; learning goal is student-friendly; evidence is observable; context choices are flexible; K-support preserves learning goal

tasks.label: Student packet
tasks.prompt: student packet has task overview; no answer key content in student packet; done-when checklist included; completion_check signal included

tasks.label: Teacher guide
tasks.prompt: teacher guide includes project_prompt, matching_bank, quick_tools, assessment_look_fors, teacher_notes

tasks.label: Assessment support
tasks.prompt: rubric/checklist is aligned; exit tickets are usable; teacher answer key is teacher audience only

embedded_supports: Do not put expected answers or teacher notes in student-facing materials.

teacher_context_guidance.title: Teacher Context Guidance
teacher_context_guidance.purpose_line: Teacher-facing guidance for choosing context families.
instructions: Choose context only after target is clear.

tasks.label: Context decision rules
tasks.prompt: Use one main context family unless intentionally cross-curricular. Do not force garden / planter / food systems into unrelated targets.

tasks.label: Local relevance
tasks.prompt: Use Kamloops/local context when it increases relevance without requiring extra research.

tasks.label: First Peoples content
tasks.prompt: Use protocol-aware language and teacher-selected/local-approved sources only. Do not invent generic First Peoples stories or knowledge.

success_criteria: Context does not add unnecessary reading load.

student_support_frame_template.title: Student Support Frame Template
student_support_frame_template.purpose_line: Reusable reduced-load support frame. This page is student-facing.
instructions: Use this frame to name the learning goal, evidence, context, words, and starting point.
Complete the done-when checklist before handing in work.

tasks.label: Before I start
tasks.prompt: Today I am learning to...

tasks.label: reference bank
tasks.prompt: Words I need and support tools I can use.

tasks.label: research planner
tasks.prompt: I can show this by creating this product or evidence.

tasks.label: My context
tasks.prompt: The context I am using is...

tasks.label: I can start with
tasks.prompt: Write your first step.

tasks.label: completion_check
tasks.prompt: I named the learning goal. I completed the required product or evidence. I used the support tools I needed. I completed the checklist or asked for help.

embedded_supports: Sentence frame: I am learning to ___.
Sentence frame: I can show this by ___.
success_criteria: done-when: I understand the learning goal.
done-when: I produced the required evidence.
done-when: I marked where I need help.

student_context_choice_frame.title: Student Context Choice Frame
student_context_choice_frame.purpose_line: Reusable student context-choice frame. This page is student-facing.
instructions: Choose one context that helps you understand the task.
Your context should still match the same learning goal.

tasks.label: Before choosing
tasks.prompt: Your teacher may assign the context if choice would make the task too confusing.

tasks.label: reference bank
tasks.prompt: Context choices: Garden / Planter / Food Systems; Design / Build / Trades; Community / Kamloops / Local Work; Outdoor Place / Land / Environment; Health / Body / Choices; Digital / Coding / Data; Consumer / Money / Market; Stories / Identity / Growth

tasks.label: research planner
tasks.prompt: I chose this context because it helps me understand the same learning goal.

tasks.label: My choice
tasks.prompt: I chose this context:

tasks.label: This context fits because
tasks.prompt: Explain why it matches the learning goal.

tasks.label: completion_check
tasks.prompt: I chose a context that matches the learning goal. I can explain why it fits. I know what product or evidence I need to create.

embedded_supports: The context should help you understand the same learning goal, not change the task.
success_criteria: done-when: I chose a context that matches the learning goal.
done-when: I can explain why it fits.
done-when: I know what product or evidence I need to create.
